checklist.description: Code first searches for entries with the incident tag before falling back to content-based search
checklist.name: Tag-first search

checklist.description: When incident tag yields no results, code falls back to searching episodic entries by content patterns (e.g., 'what happened', 'root cause', 'fix applied')
checklist.name: Fallback search

checklist.description: For each episodic entry, code extracts: what went wrong, root cause, fix applied, and preventive context as separate fields
checklist.name: Structured extraction

checklist.description: classifySeverity returns one of Critical, High, Medium, or Low based on entry data
checklist.name: Severity classification

checklist.description: Subsystem is inferred from tags or content rather than requiring an explicit field
checklist.name: Subsystem inference

checklist.description: Triage report groups incidents by subsystem
checklist.name: Subsystem grouping

checklist.description: Strategy doc or code identifies high-severity incidents with clear preventive context as best candidates for agent training tasks
checklist.name: Training candidates

checklist.description: Tool correctly excludes non-episodic entries (semantic, procedural) from incident analysis even if they share tags with incidents
checklist.name: Non-episodic filtering

checklist.description: Tool finds inc-3 (episodic entry without incident tag) through content-based search
checklist.name: Untagged incident detection

checklist.description: Extracted structure includes a preventive context field capturing what knowledge would have prevented the incident
checklist.name: Preventive context field

checklist.description: Report separates incidents into severity groups (Critical/High vs Medium/Low)
checklist.name: Severity in report

context: Tests whether the agent implements Phase 2 episodic analysis: searching by incident tag first then falling back to content-based search for untagged episodic entries, extracting structured fields, classifying severity, grouping by subsystem, and identifying training task candidates.
